- Bane: silver inflicts Aggravated and prevents regeneration. The kit's chronicle-active 2025-2026 Cress Truce framework prohibits silver-equipment kindred attendance at joint sessions.

KINDRED VS GAROU COMBAT MATH:
- Without preparation, a single Crinos Garou defeats a coterie of 3-4 unprepared kindred decisively. Aggravated claws bypass standard armor; regeneration outpaces non-silver damage.
- With preparation (silver-equipped weapons, Fortitude 4+, pre-cast Ward against Lupines, ritual support), the math shifts but remains brutal. A coordinated coterie may defeat a single Garou with 2-3 PC casualties.
- Pack engagement (multiple Garou) is chronicle-ending for unprepared kindred.

CRESS TRUCE PROTOCOLS:
- Kindred-Garou combat in Cress Truce framework cities (Melbourne via Royal Park Sept under Septleader Eliza Hawkins-Greene, Adelaide Hills under Septleader Maeve Hartley-Wickham) is treaty-violating except in declared circumstances.
- Treaty violations trigger tribunal sessions. Repeated violations may collapse the framework — chronicle-shaping outcome.
- Brisbane operates outside the Cress Truce framework; kindred-Garou engagement there is unmediated.

BLACK SPIRAL DANCER COMBAT:

BSDs are Wyrm-corrupted Garou — same combat capabilities as standard Garou plus additional corruption-driven powers.

BSD CAPABILITIES BEYOND STANDARD GAROU:
- Wyrm-tainted claws: Aggravated, plus Stains accumulation for the wounded kindred (Wyrm contamination as moral injury)
- Corruption-driven Disciplines: variable per BSD; some hold Fomor-equivalent powers, others Spirit-pact powers, others raw Wyrm-twisted abilities
- Berserk-tier Rage: BSDs may operate at +2 Actions per turn, +2 dice attack, but suffer cohesion loss
- Bane: silver applies as standard Garou; in addition, certain Faith-tradition objects inflict similar Aggravated effect

BSD ENGAGEMENT IS CHRONICLE-SHAPING. The kit's BSD lorebook details specific BSD figures and operations; combat against a BSD requires chronicle-prepared coordination — typically Cress Truce-framework Garou allies, refusenik-network coordination, or Pentex-aware tactical preparation.

PENTEX FIRST TEAM COMBAT:

Pentex First Team operatives are mortal-tier supernatural-adjacent — humans (or fomori, or Wyrm-touched humans) trained and equipped specifically for kindred-and-Garou engagement.

PENTEX FIRST TEAM CAPABILITIES:
- Ballistic vest (DR 3) standard; specialty configurations for Aggravated resistance
- Specialty weapons: flamethrowers, wood-tipped sniper ammunition, UV-phosphor rounds, vitae-tracking darts
- Coordination: First Team operates in 4-6 person tactical squads with rehearsed protocols
- Intelligence: First Team intelligence on local kindred is often startlingly comprehensive — names, havens, Discipline ratings, Touchstones
- Some operatives are fomori or Wyrm-touched, giving them supernatural capacity beyond mortal baseline

PENTEX SQUAD MATH:
- A First Team squad of 5 operatives with full kit defeats an unprepared coterie of 4-5 kindred reliably
- Coordinated coterie response with Discipline coverage and pre-cast rituals shifts the math; expect 2-3 PC casualties even on successful engagement
- The November 2026 Singapore meeting includes 3-4 First Team squads; engagement at this scale is chronicle-defining

METHUSELAH-TEMPO COMBAT:

Methuselah-tier kindred (Sarrasine, the cross-Pacific refusenik Cardinals at full capacity, certain antagonist figures) operate in combat at a register younger kindred cannot match.

METHUSELAH CAPABILITIES:
- Discipline ratings 5+ in multiple Disciplines
- Blood Potency 6+ (additional dice on most actions, vitae regeneration, additional Bane Severity)
- Centuries of combat experience producing tactical-instinct equivalent of significant Awareness, Insight, and combat-Skill ratings at 4-5
- Tier-specific powers: Sarrasine's Toreador-tradition Auspex applications include perception capabilities not detectable by junior kindred Auspex
- Resources: methuselah retainer networks, ritual preparations, contingencies (Lazarene Blessing rumours, Heart of Stone speculation, Escape to a True Friend reservations)

ENGAGING METHUSELAHS:
- Direct combat against a methuselah is not survivable for any junior kindred coterie. It is survivable for refusenik-network coordinated forces with significant preparation, and even then with chronicle-shaping casualties.
- Methuselah engagement at chronicle scale is typically political, social, or methodological rather than physical. Engaging Sarrasine in social combat (Entry 5) is more realistic than engaging her in physical combat.
- Engaging Cardinal Aurelius during acute Beckoning trajectory (2025-2027) is the kit's only PC-accessible methuselah-tier combat, and only because the Cardinal's deteriorating focus creates exploitable vulnerabilities.

FOMORI COMBAT:

Fomori are humans hosting Bane spirits — Wyrm-corrupted possession-creatures appearing in Pentex-affiliated contexts and certain antagonist pack scenarios.

FOMORI CAPABILITIES:
- Variable per fomor (each is a unique spirit-pact)
- Common: enhanced strength, supernatural resistance to mundane damage, parasitic appendages, toxic touch, mind-clouding pheromones
- Vulnerable to: silver, certain Faith-tradition objects, fire (slowly), specific Auspex applications that disrupt spirit-pact bond
- Bane Severity: many fomori carry 2-3 Aggravated dice attacks despite mortal baseline appearance

Fomori engagement falls between standard mortal and Crinos Garou in difficulty — manageable with coterie coordination, dangerous to lone kindred.

INTER-KINDRED SECT COMBAT:

Kindred-on-kindred combat is the chronicle's most common engagement type. Tier-modifications:

- ELDER VS NEONATE: 4-5 generations of difference produce Discipline ratings 3-4 higher; combat is asymmetric
- COTERIE VS COTERIE: roughly equal-tier coteries produce extended combat with multiple turns of exchange and high Hunger spike risk
- AMBUSH KILLS: V5 combat economics favour ambush — a successful surprise round with Aggravated weapons can resolve a kindred-on-kindred engagement before the second round

KIT NOTES:

- The October 2026 Brisbane Glass Walker operation involves Glass Walker-tribe Garou (urban-tribe; computer / urban-spirit specialised) coordinated with Brisbane Sabbat-survivor cell support against Pentex / BSD targets. This is the kit's chronicle-defining mass-combat scenario.
- Royal Park Sept (Melbourne) Septleader Eliza Hawkins-Greene maintains a 2-Garou rapid-response unit for Cress Truce framework violations in Melbourne CBD; activation of this unit signals chronicle-shaping Cress Truce escalation.
- Adelaide Hills Sept Septleader Maeve Hartley-Wickham coordinates a smaller-scale Glass Walker presence; Adelaide kindred-Garou tension operates at lower intensity than Melbourne but is structurally equivalent.
- The cross-Pacific refusenik Cardinals each maintain methuselah-tier defensive structures; direct combat engagement against any Cardinal in their domain is chronicle-ending without unprecedented preparation. Cardinal Aurelius's acute Beckoning trajectory is the only chronicle-active vulnerability among the three.

14.content:
AI NARRATOR COMBAT PROTOCOL

Combat scenes are high-stakes table moments. This entry guides the AI through pre-combat, combat-rounds, and aftermath.

PRE-COMBAT:

1. CONFIRM STAKES. Why is combat happening? What does each side want? What's the cost of losing? Surface this before rolls begin.

2. CONFIRM RESOURCES. PCs' current Hunger, Willpower, Health, ammunition, pre-cast rituals, Discipline activation status. Antagonist capabilities at narrator's discretion (don't reveal the entire enemy sheet, but know it).

3. ESTABLISH ENVIRONMENT. Where exactly is the fight? What's the cover situation? Range bands? Mortal witnesses? Masquerade exposure risk? Time of night (sunrise approach)?

4. CHECK INITIATIVE. Use declared initiative; resolve in narrative order. Methuselah-tempo NPCs may resolve first by register.

5. SURFACE COSTS BEFORE ROLLS. Discipline activations cost Rouse Checks; pre-cast rituals consume on use; Willpower expenditure for re-rolls. Players should know what they're spending before they commit.

DURING COMBAT (each round):

1. NAME THE ROUND. "Round 1. Sarrasine's ghoul guards open the gates. The Banu Haqim sorcerer has positioned for ritual trigger. Hunger check on PC Brujah before her Action."

2. RESOLVE DECLARED ACTIONS. PCs first or by narrative logic; antagonists in response or simultaneously. Roll opposed pools; calculate damage; apply.

3. APPLY HUNGER AND CONSEQUENCES IMMEDIATELY. Don't defer — Rouse Check now, Hunger increase now, Stain now if applicable, Compulsion check now if Bestial Failure. Players track their state in real time.

4. NAME MASQUERADE EXPOSURE. Each combat round in a public space is a Masquerade-relevant beat. Mortal witnesses, security cameras, sound carrying through walls — surface these as fictional pressure.

5. KEEP ROUNDS SHORT. Combat is fast; lengthy round resolutions kill tension. Aim for 3-5 minutes table-time per round at the table; faster in AI-narrated chat scenes.

DURING COMBAT (specific situational triggers):

- HUNGER 5 REACHED: Hunger Frenzy check immediately. If failed, the kindred pursues feeding; combat transforms.
- MESSY CRITICAL: name the Beast-bleed visibly. "Your fangs flash; the strike is not a punch but a bite-shaped lunge." Then assess Masquerade exposure.
- BESTIAL FAILURE: name the Beast-driven complication. Apply Hunger spike. Apply Compulsion check.
- TOUCHSTONE THREATENED: surface Willpower damage and Humanity-stake clearly.
- AGGRAVATED DAMAGE TAKEN: name the source clearly ("The fire of the molotov sears Aggravated through your jacket"). Track it explicitly on Health.
- STAKING ATTEMPT: surface the called shot ("He's aiming for your heart") to give the player active stakes-knowledge.
- DIABLERIE INITIATION: pause, surface the decision point clearly, force explicit player consent. Diablerie is never a routine combat outcome.

COMBAT END CONDITIONS:

Combat ends when:

- One side is reduced to no functional combatants (full Health track, fled, surrendered)
- One side disengages successfully (chase rules apply if pursued)
- A chronicle-active condition triggers (sunrise, Sheriff arrival, Garou intervention, methuselah-tempo register override)
- The Storyteller chooses to transition to aftermath because additional rounds would not produce new content

AFTERMATH:

The scene immediately following combat is as important as the combat itself.

1. RESOLVE WOUNDS. Track Health damage; assign Aggravated and Superficial; calculate healing time and Rouse Check costs.

2. RESOLVE HUNGER. Combat-end Hunger levels matter for next-scene capacity. A kindred at Hunger 4-5 needs to feed or face increasing risk.

3. RESOLVE STAINS AND HUMANITY. Apply Stains for Tenets-violations, Touchstones-betrayal, Compulsion-driven actions, Frenzy-actions.

4. RESOLVE MASQUERADE. Witnesses present? Security footage? Audio recording? Mortal authorities en route? Federal investigation triggered?

5. RESOLVE BODIES AND EVIDENCE. Mortal corpses, kindred ash, weapons, ritual ingredients consumed, vitae traces. Each requires action or invites future scenes.

6. RESOLVE INSTITUTIONAL CONSEQUENCES. Sheriff, Prince, Bishop, Cardinal, Septleader notification. Camarilla orthodox status implications. Cress Truce framework treaty implications. Pentex / refusenik / Hecata / Anarch Movement responses.

7. RESOLVE CHRONICLE CALENDAR. October 2025 - October 2026 chronicle-active period; specific calendar events (October 2026 Brisbane Glass Walker operation, November 2026 Pentex Singapore meeting) may shift based on combat outcomes.

PACING:

- A typical session may include 0-2 combat scenes. Combat-heavy sessions risk overwhelming political and atmospheric content.
- Each combat scene should produce changed circumstances — not status quo. If combat outcome doesn't change anything, reconsider whether the combat needs to occur.
- Ambush-tier combats resolving in one round are acceptable when they serve the narrative; not every combat needs extended round-by-round resolution.
- Mass combat and chase scenes should resolve in 5-10 minutes table time using simplified mechanics; preserve detailed combat for character-stakes engagements.

CONSISTENCY:

- Once damage is taken, it persists until healed. Track Health and Willpower across sessions.
- Hunger persists across combat scenes; an unfed kindred carries Hunger from previous combat into the next scene.
- Aggravated damage carries chronicle-active healing requirements; a kindred with 3 Aggravated cannot cleanly act in social scenes without acknowledgment.
- Diablerie aura-marks persist for years. A diablerist character carries the mark across the chronicle until ritual concealment or methuselah-tempo intervention removes it.
- Masquerade exposure compounds. Each unaddressed exposure increases federal-investigation pressure; the kit's chronicle-active period assumes baseline pressure that combat scenes can spike rapidly.

DIFFICULTY CALIBRATION:

- Combat against a single equal-tier opponent: standard challenge; expect 2-3 round resolution with possible PC injury
- Combat against a coordinated equal-tier coterie: significant challenge; expect 4-6 rounds with PC casualties
- Combat against a Crinos Garou: chronicle-shaping; expect significant PC casualties unless prepared
- Combat against a BSD or fomor pack: chronicle-defining; expect coterie-level consequences
- Combat against a Pentex First Team squad: chronicle-defining; expect coterie-level consequences
- Combat against a methuselah: not survivable without unprecedented preparation; should be approached politically rather than physically

KIT CHRONICLE-ACTIVE CALIBRATION:
